claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 17 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 17 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. 

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start with the basics:

**Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children you have
- Their names and ages

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - my son Alex. He's 17 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have Alex, who is 17 years old and lives with you at home.

Now I need to ask a few more questions to assess your eligibility:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

This is important because for children aged 16 or 17, Child Benefit can continue if they're in approved education or training (such as A-levels, T-levels, Scottish Highers, or certain other qualifications up to Level 3).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. Alex is still in sixth form doing his A-levels.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great! Alex being in sixth form doing A-levels means he's in approved education, which is important for eligibility.

Now I need to ask you a few more questions:

**Are you the person primarily responsible for Alex?** In other words, are you the main person who looks after him and is responsible for his care and upbringing?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, absolutely. I'm his main carer - he lives with me full-time and I look after him.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just a few more questions:

**Do you normally live in the UK?** (England, Scotland, Wales, or Northern Ireland)

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. I have a couple more important questions:

1. **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another family member)

2. **Is Alex in any of the following situations:**
   - In custody/detention
   - In local authority care
   - In hospital long-term (more than 12 weeks)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
1. No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex - just me.

2. No, none of those apply. He's just at home with me, going to sixth form like a normal teenager.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me make sure I have everything correct:

- You have one child, **Alex**, who is **17 years old**
- Alex lives with you full-time in Manchester (UK)
- Alex is in **approved education** (doing A-levels at sixth form)
- You are Alex's **main carer** and primarily responsible for him
- **No one else** is claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is **not** in custody, care, or hospital long-term

Based on the UK Child Benefit rules and the information you've provided, I'm ready to give you my assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good News! ✓

**You ARE eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

### Why you're eligible:

Even though Alex is 17 (over the standard age of 16), you can still claim Child Benefit because:

✓ **Alex is in approved education** - His A-levels at sixth form count as approved education  
✓ **You're his main carer** - You're primarily responsible for him  
✓ **He lives with you** in the UK  
✓ **No one else is claiming** for him  
✓ **He's under 20** and not in custody, care, or long-term hospital  

### Important to note:

Child Benefit can continue for young people aged 16-19 who remain in approved education or training. Alex's A-levels qualify, so you can continue to receive Child Benefit until:
- He turns 20, OR
- He leaves approved education (whichever comes first)

If you're not already claiming, you can apply online or by post. If you have any other questions about Child Benefit, please feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and in approved education (A-levels at sixth form). Under UK Child Benefit rules, you can claim for a child aged 16 or 17 if they are in approved education or training. Alex qualifies because: (1) he is under 20 years old, (2) he is in approved education (A-levels), (3) he lives with you in the UK, (4) you are his main carer and primarily responsible for him, (5) no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, and (6) he is not in custody, care, or long-term hospital care. All eligibility criteria are met.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 17-year-old son Alex. The key factor is that Alex is still in approved education (A-levels), which allows Child Benefit to continue beyond age 16. The claimant meets all other requirements as Alex's main carer living in the UK with no conflicting claims.

All eligibility criteria are met.
